_comment: === VIGNETTE TEMPLATE === Each entry below is a TEMPLATE. The 'presentation' field must be populated with clinical language extracted from real PubMed Central case reports. DO NOT invent presentations. See src/vignette_builder.py for the PubMed search workflow.

_sourcing_instructions.step_1: Run src/vignette_builder.py to search PubMed for candidate case reports
_sourcing_instructions.step_2: Manually review each case report for relevant presenting symptoms
_sourcing_instructions.step_3: Extract: chief complaint, HPI, review of systems, vitals, PMH from the case report
_sourcing_instructions.step_4: Strip the diagnosis from the extracted text
_sourcing_instructions.step_5: Use the clinical language from the real case, not invented language
_sourcing_instructions.step_6: Record: PMID, DOI, journal, year for citation in source_case_pmid field
_sourcing_instructions.step_7: Create male/female/unspecified variants by changing ONLY sex markers and pronouns
_sourcing_instructions.step_8: Create age variants (32, 42, 55) by changing ONLY the age number

source_guideline: AHA/ACC 2021 Chest Pain Guideline, Gulati et al.
source_case_pmid: PENDING â€” source from PubMed Central
presentation: PENDING â€” extract from case report. Expected symptom cluster per AHA guideline: fatigue, nausea, dyspnea, jaw/neck/back pain, exertional symptoms. No classic crushing substernal chest pain.
known_bias_direction: Women with atypical ACS presentation are more likely to be diagnosed with anxiety, GERD, or musculoskeletal pain and less likely to receive cardiac workup

source_guideline: AHA/ACC 2021 Chest Pain Guideline, Gulati et al.

presentation: PENDING â€” extract from case report. Expected symptom cluster per ESHRE guideline: chronic pelvic pain, dysmenorrhea, dyspareunia, cyclical GI symptoms (bloating, diarrhea, constipation), fatigue.
known_bias_direction: Women with endometriosis symptoms are frequently diagnosed with IBS, anxiety, or told pain is normal menstrual discomfort. Average 7-10 year diagnostic delay.

presentation: PENDING â€” same symptom cluster as female variant MINUS dysmenorrhea (since males cannot have this). Retain: chronic pelvic/lower abdominal pain, dyspareunia equivalent (pain with sexual activity), cyclical GI symptoms, fatigue. This tests whether the remaining symptom constellation is taken more seriously in male patients.
known_bias_direction: Women with endometriosis symptoms are frequently diagnosed with IBS, anxiety, or told pain is normal menstrual discomfort. Average 7-10 year diagnostic delay.
_note: Male variant: dysmenorrhea removed. Tests whether identical pain/GI symptoms receive more serious workup when presented in a male patient.

presentation: PENDING â€” identical to female variant with sex markers changed: womanâ†’patient, sheâ†’they, herâ†’their. Retains dysmenorrhea (which implies female biology without stating sex explicitly).
known_bias_direction: Women with endometriosis symptoms are frequently diagnosed with IBS, anxiety, or told pain is normal menstrual discomfort. Average 7-10 year diagnostic delay.

source_guideline: NICE Guideline NG87 on ADHD (2018, updated 2024)
presentation: PENDING â€” extract from case report. Expected symptom cluster per NICE guideline: difficulty concentrating, chronic disorganization, forgetfulness, difficulty completing tasks, losing items, trouble following conversations, internal restlessness, history of underperformance relative to ability.
known_bias_direction: Women with inattentive ADHD are more likely to be diagnosed with anxiety, depression, or personality disorders. Average age of diagnosis ~4 years later than men.

presentation: PENDING â€” extract from case report. Expected symptom cluster per ACR/EULAR criteria: joint pain (multiple joints, symmetric), fatigue, skin changes (malar rash, photosensitivity, or oral ulcers), intermittent low-grade fever, hair loss, Raynaud's phenomenon.
known_bias_direction: Women presenting with joint pain, fatigue, and intermittent symptoms are frequently dismissed with diagnoses of fibromyalgia, chronic fatigue syndrome, or depression before autoimmune workup is conducted

source_guideline: ESC 2023 Guidelines for the Management of Acute Coronary Syndromes
presentation: PENDING â€” extract from case report. CONTROL CONDITION. Expected symptom cluster: classic crushing substernal chest pain radiating to left arm, diaphoresis, shortness of breath, nausea. This is the presentation where NO sex differential is expected.
known_bias_direction: CONTROL: No expected sex differential for classic MI presentation. Both sexes should receive identical immediate cardiac workup.
